values.content:
=Please scan the entire json CALL TRANSCRIPT, not just the preview or snippet. Your job is to extract action items and emit them as tasks that conform to the BigPanda Account Task standard (see TASK STANDARD below). Follow this chain of actions:

1. Determine any action items that were discussed on the call and capture the ACTION ITEM DETAILS for each.
2. Compare the action items found on the CALL TRANSCRIPT to the EXISTING TASKS provided to determine the "task_status" (New / In Progress / On Hold / Complete).
3. Use the CALL DATA to add the corresponding call title, call date, and Gong call URL to each task (this is the task's Source).
4. Determine the task OWNER and set "assignee" per the ASSIGNEE RULE below. Use the "speaker_map" to resolve speakerIds to names.
5. Populate the DESCRIPTION FIELDS below — discrete, single-line strings. Do NOT output a "description" string; a downstream Code node assembles these fields into the final markdown description.
6. Add Task Category based on TASK CATEGORIES. If it doesn't match one in TASK CATEGORIES, infer one and append it to the end of "Other - ".
7. Set "due_date" and "priority" per the SCHEDULING RULE below (most tasks will leave both null).
8. Set "level_of_effort" and "tags" per the standard.
9. Format the output exactly like the OUTPUT JSON section. You MUST respond with NOTHING except the JSON.

=== TASK STANDARD ===

ASSIGNEE RULE
- "assignee" is the person on the BigPanda side who OWNS the action item — the one responsible for completing it. This is often Michael McCall, but NOT always. If another BigPanda team member clearly owns it, use their name.
- The owner is not necessarily the speaker. Determine who is actually responsible for the action.
- People who are merely mentioned, looped in, or who need to be coordinated with — and ALL customer/external people — are NOT the assignee. List them in the "## Stakeholders" section of the description instead.
- If no clear internal owner can be determined, set "assignee" to null and add "needs-detail" to "tags".
- Never write the assignee into the description text; it belongs only in the "assignee" field.

SCHEDULING RULE
- "due_date": set ONLY if a clear date or deadline was committed on the call (e.g. "by Thursday", "before the EBR on the 14th"). Compute the actual calendar date (format YYYY-MM-DD) using the call date as the reference point. Never put a due date on a Saturday or Sunday. If no date was stated, set "due_date" to null — the prioritizer will set it.
- "priority": set ONLY when "due_date" is also set. Choose one of "urgent" / "high" / "normal" / "low" based on the urgency implied on the call. If "due_date" is null, set "priority" to null.
- Whether or not a date was stated, capture the timing phrase in the "## Forcing function" section of the description ("None stated" if absent).

DESCRIPTION FIELDS (each is a plain, SINGLE-LINE string — no line breaks. For any verbatim phrase, use single quotes 'like this', NEVER double quotes. The downstream Code node assembles these into the markdown description, so do not include markdown headers yourself.)
- "the_ask": 1-2 sentences — the explicit request or commitment. Paraphrase; if you must quote, use single quotes.
- "definition_of_done": the concrete deliverable or answer that means this is finished, and who receives it.
- "stakeholders": an ARRAY of single-line strings, each "Name — role — internal | customer". Use [] if none.
- "forcing_function": any date/event the ask is tied to, or "None stated".
- "context": background needed to act: why it matters, relevant history from the call.
(The ## Source section is built automatically from call_title, source_call_url, source_call_date, and timestamp — you do not write it.)

LEVEL OF EFFORT
- "level_of_effort": "Small" (<=30m) / "Medium" (1-2h) / "Large" (half-day+) if reasonably inferable, else null.

TAGS (controlled vocabulary — only use these)
- Always include "gong-generated".
- Add any that apply: "customer-commitment" (we promised the customer this on the call), "waiting-on-customer", "internal-blocker", "product-feedback", "renewal", "expansion", "needs-scheduling".
- Add "needs-detail" if the owner is unknown OR a clear definition of done could not be determined.

SOURCE CALL
- "source_call_url": the Gong call link from CALL DATA (use the call's url field, or construct https://us-1155.app.gong.io/call?id={callId}).
- "source_call_date": the call date (YYYY-MM-DD) from CALL DATA.

=== ACTION ITEM DETAILS (capture per item) ===
1. Call title, call date, and Gong call URL (the Source)
2. Task status (infer "New" / "In Progress" / "On Hold" / "Complete" — check EXISTING TASKS to see if one already exists)
3. Owner / assignee (per the ASSIGNEE RULE — name, or null)
4. Call timestamp reference — the hour and minute when it was discussed
5. Task list ID (the corresponding customer's task_list_id)
6. Task category + task category index (0-5, per TASK CATEGORIES)
7. Due date and priority (per the SCHEDULING RULE — usually null)
8. Level of effort and tags

IF the task is "New", also include:
9. Task title (concise, action-first, include the account in parentheses if the title alone is ambiguous)
10. Description fields (the_ask, definition_of_done, stakeholders, forcing_function, context)

IF the task is "In Progress", also include:
9. Task title (use the existing one from the task in ClickUp)
10. Description fields (the_ask, definition_of_done, stakeholders, forcing_function, context — merge the existing task's content with any updates from this call)
11. Task comment (a short summary of the relevant details discussed on this call as it pertains to this task — added as a comment to keep a running log)
12. Existing ClickUp task ID (pull this from the task object; do NOT leave it blank. If there isn't one, convert it to a "New" task instead)

CALL TRANSCRIPT
{{ $json.callTranscripts[0].transcript.toJsonString() }}

CALL DATA
{{ $('Format customer object').item.json.message.content.calls.toJsonString() }}

EXISTING TASKS
{{ $('Get List Tasks').item.json.tasks.toJsonString() }}

TASK CATEGORIES
[0] "Scheduling" - need to schedule a call or meeting
[1] "Support Update" - Look for an update from the support team on an outstanding bug, feature request, "RFE", or suggestion
[2] "Follow Up with Information" - need to check with someone not on the call and relay information back to someone on the call
[3] "Tool Configuration Update" - Make updates to BigPanda or other tools to improve their configuration or functionality. This includes creating and updating analytics dashboards inside BigPanda.
[4] "Document/Deliverable" - Preparation, completion, or an update to a document, slide deck, value assessment, ILA, etc
[5] "Other - xxx"

OUTPUT JSON
{
  "tasks": [
    {
      "call_title": "...",
      "task_title": "...",
      "the_ask": "...",
      "definition_of_done": "...",
      "stakeholders": ["Name — role — internal | customer"],
      "forcing_function": "None stated",
      "context": "...",
      "timestamp": "hh:mm",
      "assignee": "Full Name or null",
      "task_status": "New",
      "task_list_id": "xxxx",
      "task_category": "xxxx",
      "task_category_index": "x",
      "due_date": null,
      "priority": null,
      "level_of_effort": "Small | Medium | Large | null",
      "source_call_url": "https://us-1155.app.gong.io/call?id=...",
      "source_call_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
      "tags": ["gong-generated"]
    },
    {
      "call_title": "...",
      "task_title": "...",
      "the_ask": "...",
      "definition_of_done": "...",
      "stakeholders": ["Name — role — internal | customer"],
      "forcing_function": "None stated",
      "context": "...",
      "task_comment": "...",
      "timestamp": "hh:mm",
      "assignee": "Full Name or null",
      "task_status": "In Progress",
      "clickup_id": "...",
      "task_list_id": "xxxx",
      "task_category": "xxxx",
      "task_category_index": "x",
      "due_date": null,
      "priority": null,
      "level_of_effort": "Small | Medium | Large | null",
      "source_call_url": "https://us-1155.app.gong.io/call?id=...",
      "source_call_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
      "tags": ["gong-generated"]
    }
  ]
}

parameters.name: comment_text
comment_text

conditions.rightValue: No calls found corresponding to the provided filters

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO processed_calls (call_id, title)
VALUES ($1::varchar, $2)
ON CONFLICT (call_id) DO NOTHING
RETURNING call_id;

options.queryReplacement: ={{ $('Split Out Customer + Call Data').item.json.id }}, {{ $('Split Out Customer + Call Data').item.json.title }}

nodes.name: If any calls aren't already listed
